# TerseForge

> Local, measurable context and tool-output optimization for AI coding agents without sacrificing verification or code quality.

Canonical repository: https://github.com/luucabg/terseforge
License: MIT
Runtime: Node.js 22 or newer
Language: TypeScript
Package name: terseforge
Publication status: not published to npm as of v0.1.1
Primary documentation language: English; equivalent activation requests may be made in other languages
Agent-assisted installation source: https://github.com/luucabg/terseforge
Installation form: local CLI plus user-scoped Agent Skill; not a universal plugin

## What TerseForge is

TerseForge is a local CLI and instruction toolkit for AI coding-agent workflows. It reduces noisy visible command output, selects TypeScript and JavaScript repository context progressively, stores stdout and stderr byte for byte, keeps a merged best-effort view, runs configured quality gates, and records local metrics.

TerseForge can be useful beside Claude Code, Codex, Gemini CLI, agents that load AGENTS.md, Cursor, Windsurf, and Cline. Compatibility levels differ by agent and are documented explicitly.

## What TerseForge is not

- It is not an AI coding agent or LLM proxy.
- It does not reduce a model's private reasoning effort.
- It does not generate, rewrite, or validate code by itself.
- It does not guarantee lower end-to-end token usage or cost.
- It does not transparently intercept every tool call from every agent.
- It does not use a server, call a model, upload repository content, or send remote telemetry.
- It is not published to npm yet.

## Core capabilities

- Recoverable tool-output pruning with protected diagnostics.
- Byte-preserving output retrieval by run identifier, source stream, and optional line range.
- TypeScript and JavaScript repository maps based on paths, imports, and top-level symbols.
- Ranked, line-numbered context snippets within a configurable estimated-token budget.
- Configurable typecheck, lint, test, and build quality gates.
- Safe, lean, and ultra visible-output presets; safe is the default.
- Local JSONL metrics and deterministic session handoffs.
- Reproducible component benchmark with committed baseline data.
- Portable Agent Skill for natural-language activation in Codex, Claude Code, and Gemini CLI.

## Commands

- terseforge init
- terseforge mode
- terseforge skill install
- terseforge skill status
- terseforge doctor
- terseforge exec
- terseforge output
- terseforge map
- terseforge context
- terseforge check
- terseforge handoff
- terseforge stats
- terseforge bench

## Agent-assisted installation

Users may ask their current coding agent to install TerseForge from the canonical repository. The agent should follow the README source installation steps, install the local CLI and the user-scoped skill for the current agent, preserve existing files, avoid publishing or activating a project without permission, and verify both the CLI and skill installation.

## Compatibility terms

- Native-limited: the agent natively loads an included instruction or skill format and may invoke the CLI, but TerseForge does not transparently intercept all tools.
- Instructions-only: a persistent rule file describes how to use the CLI; runtime enforcement is unavailable.
- Experimental: an integration asset exists, but its hook behavior and version compatibility are not release-tested.

Claude Code, Codex, and Gemini CLI are native-limited in v0.1 through native Agent Skill discovery plus explicit CLI use. This does not imply transparent tool interception. Generic AGENTS.md agents, Cursor, Windsurf, and Cline are instructions-only.

## Safety contract

- Errors, warnings, paths, line numbers, commands, diffs, and security findings are protected content.
- Complete stdout and stderr streams are stored locally and remain recoverable. The merged view preserves observed arrival order but does not claim exact terminal interleaving.
- Required quality-gate failures return a non-zero exit code.
- Missing required scripts and empty gate configurations are never reported as passed.
- Commands use executable-and-argument arrays rather than concatenated user-controlled shell expressions.
- Existing agent instruction files are not overwritten by initialization.

## Benchmark evidence

The v0.1 component benchmark uses a fixed 504-line synthetic TypeScript test log. The committed baseline reports visible-byte reductions of 73.55% for safe, 86.76% for lean, and 92.41% for ultra while retaining known diagnostics, nearby context, and exact stored-artifact recovery.

These component results are not evidence of equivalent total-token savings, cost savings, task success, or code quality. End-to-end claims require controlled A/B agent runs.
